'''Junior Nsemba''' is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]] and {{rlnt|England}} at international level.

==Background==
Born in [[Wigan]], Nsemba is of Cameroonian heritage, both his parents emigrated to Wigan from Cameroon prior to his birth.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Jones |first=Chris |title=Highly-rated Wigan Warriors prospect holds Cameroon ambition as Rugby League reaches country |url=https://www.totalrl.com/highly-rated-wigan-warriors-prospect-holds-cameroon-ambition-as-rugby-league-reaches-country/ |website=Total Rugby League |date=5 February 2023 |access-date=30 January 2025 |language=en-US}}</ref> He began playing junior [[rugby league]] at [[Wigan St Judes]] from the age of 11.<ref>{{cite web |title=Wigan Warriors' Junior Nsemba filling mentor's boots after choosing rugby over football |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/26126/13148716/challenge-cup-final-wigan-warriors-junior-nsemba-filling-mentors-boots-after-choosing-rugby-over-football |website=Sky Sports |access-date=2 March 2025 |date=6 June 2024}}</ref>

He is the nephew and cousin of former footballers [[Rigobert Song|Rigobert]] and [[Alex Song]], respectively.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Newsum |first1=Matt |title=Rising star Nsemba ready for Wigan's Wembley date |date=7 June 2024 |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cqqq994l5vdo |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=4 March 2026}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Wigan Warriors===
In August 2022, Nsemba made his [[Super League]] début for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Wigan boss Matt Peet predicts bright future for Junior Nsemba following new long-term deal |url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-boss-matt-peet-predicts-bright-future-for-junior-nsemba-following-new-long-term-deal-4381011 |website=Wigan Today |access-date=2 March 2025 |date=22 October 2023}}</ref> In 2023, Nsemba came off the bench to score his first career try in an 18–14 victory against [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the 6th round of the Challenge Cup.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62661463|title=Super League: Hull KR 38-28 Wigan Warriors - Home side hold on despite late flurry|work=BBC Sport }}</ref> Nsemba made 15 senior appearances for Wigan across the [[2023 Wigan Warriors season|2023 season]], signing a four year contract at the end of the season in which Wigan won the [[League Leaders' Shield]] and [[Super League Grand Final]].<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-warriors-lock-in-young-gun-junior-nsemba-on-long-term-contract | title=Wigan Warriors lock in young gun on long-term contract: 'He is an outstanding young man and talented player' | date=22 October 2023 }}</ref> On 8 June 2024, Nsemba played in Wigan's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] victory over [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=8 June 2024 }}</ref> Nsemba made the [[Super League Dream Team]] for the [[2024 Super League season]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-09-22 |title=Super League Dream Team 2024: Wigan provide four representatives |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c78de9v0q21o |access-date=2025-01-30 |website=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> Two days later, he signed a new six-year contract, keeping him at the club until 2030.<ref>{{Cite news |title=Junior Nsemba: Wigan Warriors second-row signs new six-year contract with Super League champions |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12215/13221670/junior-nsemba-wigan-warriors-second-row-signs-new-six-year-contract-with-super-league-champions |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240926134108/https://www.skysports.com/amp/rugby-league/news/12215/13221670/junior-nsemba-wigan-warriors-second-row-signs-new-six-year-contract-with-super-league-champions |archive-date=2024-09-26 |access-date=2025-01-30 |work=Sky Sports |language=en-GB |url-status=live }}</ref>

On 12 October 2024, Nsemba played in Wigan's 9–2 [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

On 9 October 2025, Nsemba played in Wigan's 24–6 [[2025 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 30 May 2026, Nsemba played in Wigan's 2026 Challenge Cup final victory against Hull Kingston Rovers. Nsemba was controversially not sent off in the second half of the match for a tackle on Hull KR's Bill Leyland. Hull Kingston Rovers head coach Willie Peters described the tackle as "one of the worst he had ever seen".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/01/challenge-cup-crowds-are-dwindling-but-rugby-league-must-save-wembley-relationship|title=Challenge Cup crowds are dwindling but rugby league must save Wembley relationship|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

==International==
On 2 Nov 2024 he made his début for {{rlnt|England}} in the 34–16 win over {{rlnt|Samoa}}.
